heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 1. Scope and goals > 1.1 AMP-facing coverage contract
statement:
- BadGuys MUST NOT claim full runner coverage merely from a passing partial or
  historical matrix.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 1. Scope and goals > 1.1 AMP-facing coverage contract
statement:
- New runner-facing capability added to scripts/am_patch.py or its normative
  specification MUST be assigned to one of the capability groups in this
  document before related coverage is claimed.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 1. Scope and goals > 1.1 AMP-facing coverage contract
text:
- Capability-based coverage does not require enumerating every flag
  combination or every policy permutation.

title: 1.1.1 Expensive gate coverage discipline
heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 1. Scope and goals > 1.1.1 Expensive gate coverage discipline
BadGuys Specification > 1. Scope and goals > 1.1.1 Expensive gate coverage discipline
statement:
- For expensive runner gate families whose execution cost can dominate suite
  runtime, BadGuys MUST prefer one explicit normative sentinel path over
  repeated incidental triggering in unrelated tests.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 1. Scope and goals > 1.1.1 Expensive gate coverage discipline
statement:
- When BadGuys claims coverage for the pytest gate family in G3, it MUST
  provide at least one dedicated test whose central evaluation proves that the
  pytest gate executed.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 1. Scope and goals > 1.1.1 Expensive gate coverage discipline
statement:
- The dedicated pytest path MUST constrain runner pytest scope to a targeted
  repo-relative test file rather than the full default tests target set.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 1. Scope and goals > 1.1.1 Expensive gate coverage discipline
statement:
- Tests whose primary assertion is not pytest coverage MUST NOT rely on
  incidental pytest execution for correctness claims. When such a test would
  otherwise trigger pytest only as a side effect, its runner invocation SHOULD
  pass --skip-pytest.

text: - repo_root: the Git repository root (parent directory of "badguys/").
- suite run: one invocation of "python3 badguys/badguys.py ...".
- test: one file in "badguys/tests/" with the ".bdg" extension.
- plan: an ordered list of steps returned by a test.
- commit test: a test declaring makes_commit=true.
- config: a TOML file providing BadGuys configuration.

- capability group: one normative runner-facing coverage area defined in
  section 1.1.

- observability primitive: a step capability whose only purpose is to expose
  deterministic data to central evaluation.

title: 3. Entry points and CLI
heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI

title: 3.1 Wrapper entry point
heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.1 Wrapper entry point
BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.1 Wrapper entry point
text: "badguys/badguys.py" is a thin wrapper that:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.1 Wrapper entry point
text: 1) ensures repo_root is on sys.path

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.1 Wrapper entry point
text: 2) delegates to "badguys.run_suite.main(argv)"

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.1 Wrapper entry point
statement: Implementations MUST preserve this behavior to keep imports deterministic.

title: 3.2 Canonical invocation
heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.2 Canonical invocation
BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.2 Canonical invocation
text: From repo_root:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.2 Canonical invocation
text: - python3 badguys/badguys.py [OPTIONS]

title: 3.3 CLI options (normative)
heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.3 CLI options (normative)
BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.3 CLI options (normative)
statement: BadGuys MUST support the following CLI options and semantics:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.3 CLI options (normative)
statement:
- --config PATH
  - repo-relative path to a TOML config file
  - default: "badguys/config.toml"
  - the selected config file MUST be the single source of truth for suite-global
    behavior, runner-start recipes, and [evaluation], including [suite], [runner],
    [lock], [guard], [filters], [recipes.tests.<test_id>.steps.<step_index>] for
    runner-start keys only, and [evaluation]

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.3 CLI options (normative)
text:
- --commit-limit N
  - overrides [suite].commit_limit from config for this run

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.3 CLI options (normative)
BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.3 CLI options (normative)
text:
- console verbosity short flags (mutually exclusive):
  - -q => quiet
  - -n => normal
  - -v => verbose
  - -d => debug
  These flags override [suite].console_verbosity for this run.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.3 CLI options (normative)
BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.3 CLI options (normative)
BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.3 CLI options (normative)
text:
- --suite-jail / --no-suite-jail
  - --suite-jail forces BadGuys suite jail on for this run
  - --no-suite-jail forces BadGuys suite jail off for this run
  - these flags override [suite].suite_jail from config for this run
  - they control only the BadGuys suite-level jail and MUST NOT modify or reinterpret the AM Patch Runner patch_jail behavior

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.3 CLI options (normative)
text:
- --include NAME (repeatable)
  - add NAME to the include filter set for this run

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.3 CLI options (normative)
text:
- --exclude NAME (repeatable)
  - add NAME to the exclude filter set for this run

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.3 CLI options (normative)
text:
- --list-tests
  - list the discovered and selected tests (after applying include/exclude and guard rules) and exit

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.3 CLI options (normative)
text: Exit codes:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.3 CLI options (normative)
text: - 0 on overall SUCCESS

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.3 CLI options (normative)
text: - 1 on overall FAIL

title: 3.4 Resolution precedence (normative)
heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.4 Resolution precedence (normative)
BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.4 Resolution precedence (normative)
text: For all scalar settings (runner_verbosity, console_verbosity, log_verbosity, per_run_logs_post_run, suite_jail, commit_limit):

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.4 Resolution precedence (normative)
statement: 1) CLI explicit override MUST win

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.4 Resolution precedence (normative)
text: 2) config value is next

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.4 Resolution precedence (normative)
text: 3) default is last

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.4 Resolution precedence (normative)
text: For include/exclude:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.4 Resolution precedence (normative)
statement: - the effective include list MUST be: config.filters.include + CLI --include (in that order)

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.4 Resolution precedence (normative)
statement: - the effective exclude list MUST be: config.filters.exclude + CLI --exclude (in that order)

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.4 Resolution precedence (normative)
text: If both include and exclude are provided:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.4 Resolution precedence (normative)
statement: - include MUST be applied first (restrict to include set)

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 3. Entry points and CLI > 3.4 Resolution precedence (normative)
statement: - exclude MUST be applied second (remove excluded names)

text: Default config path: badguys/config.toml

statement:
The config file MUST be valid TOML. Missing top-level tables MUST be treated as empty.
Unknown keys MAY exist but MUST NOT change behavior unless explicitly defined by this specification.
Behavior-changing configuration for BadGuys is split deterministically between this TOML file
and per-test .bdg files. Known TOML-owned behavior-changing tables are: [suite], [runner],
[lock], [guard], [filters], runner-start step recipes under [recipes.tests.*.steps.*],
and [evaluation].

text:
- issue_id (string)
  - default issue id used by tests
  - default if missing: "666"

- runner_cmd (array of strings)
  - argv for invoking the AM Patch Runner
  - default if missing: ["python3", "scripts/am_patch.py"]

statement:
- runner_verbosity (string)
  - one of: debug, verbose, normal, quiet
  - default if missing: "quiet"
  - when non-empty, MUST be appended to runner_cmd as: --verbosity=<mode>

- console_verbosity (string)
  - one of: debug, verbose, normal, quiet
  - default if missing: "normal"
  - MAY be overridden by -q/-n/-v/-d

text:
- log_verbosity (string)
  - one of: debug, verbose, normal, quiet
  - default if missing: "normal"

- patches_dir (string)
  - repo-relative directory used by tests to store patch artifacts
  - default if missing: "patches"

- suite_jail (bool)
  - default if missing: true
  - controls whether the BadGuys suite body runs inside a bwrap jail
  - MAY be overridden by --suite-jail / --no-suite-jail
  - when true, BadGuys MUST execute the suite body against a writable issue-scoped jail repo at patches/badguys_suite_jail/issue_<issue_id>/repo
  - when false, BadGuys MUST execute the suite body against the live repo root
  - this key controls only the BadGuys suite-level jail and MUST NOT alter AM Patch Runner patch_jail behavior

statement:
- logs_dir (string)
  - repo-relative directory for per-test artifacts
  - default if missing: "patches/badguys_logs"
  - NOTE (normative): the engine MUST delete this directory at the start of each suite run, then recreate it

text:
- per_run_logs_post_run (string)
  - one of: delete_all, keep_all, delete_successful
  - default if missing: "keep_all"
  - controls what happens to per-test artifacts in logs_dir after the suite run completes
  - delete_all: delete logs_dir entirely
  - keep_all: keep all per-test artifacts
  - delete_successful: delete per-test artifact directories for tests that PASSED;
    keep artifacts for failed or unexecuted tests

statement:
- copy_runner_log (bool)
  - default if missing: false
  - if true, and if the runner IPC result includes log_path, BadGuys MAY copy that artifact into
    the per-test artifacts directory
  - if false, BadGuys MUST NOT copy log_path (runner logs are human-readable)

- write_subprocess_stdio (bool)
  - default if missing: false
  - if true, BadGuys MAY write captured subprocess stdout/stderr into per-test artifacts
  - if false, BadGuys MUST NOT write subprocess stdout/stderr into per-test artifacts

text:
- central_log_pattern (string)
  - repo-relative path pattern (format string)
  - supports {run_id}
  - default if missing: "patches/badguys_{run_id}.log"

- commit_limit (int)
  - maximum number of selected tests with makes_commit=true
  - default if missing: 1

statement:
- full_runner_tests (array of strings)
  - list of test_ids that are allowed to invoke the AM Patch Runner without --test-mode
  - default if missing: []
  - each entry MUST be an existing discovered test_id; otherwise the suite MUST FAIL before execution
  - duplicates MUST be rejected (suite MUST FAIL before execution)

text:
- path (string)
  - lock file path (repo-relative)
  - default if missing: "patches/badguys.lock"

- ttl_seconds (int)
  - lock stale threshold used by on_conflict=steal
  - default if missing: 3600

- on_conflict (string)
  - "fail" or "steal"
  - default if missing: "fail"
  - semantics are defined in section 9 (Locking)

statement:
- require_guard_test (bool)
  - default if missing: true
  - if true, the suite MUST enforce that guard_test_name is present after filtering

text:
- guard_test_name (string)
  - default if missing: "test_000_test_mode_smoke"
  - defines the required guard test name when require_guard_test=true

statement:
- abort_on_guard_fail (bool)
  - default if missing: true
  - if true, a failing guard MUST stop the run immediately (FAIL-fast)

text:
- include (array of strings)
  - default if missing: []
  - default include list (see precedence rules in 3.4)

- exclude (array of strings)
  - default if missing: []
  - default exclude list (see precedence rules in 3.4)
Per-test .bdg files define logical test subjects and their repo-relative target paths.

- structure:
  - [subjects.<subject_id>]
  - required key: relpath (string, repo-relative path)
Rules (normative):
- subject_id is a logical identifier used by assets and non-runner steps in the same .bdg file

statement: - relpath MUST be repo-relative
- relpath entries MUST be read from the owning .bdg file, not from [subjects] in TOML
- if a referenced subject is missing, the suite MUST FAIL deterministically before execution

text: Recipe authority is split deterministically between .bdg materialization data and TOML runner-start data.

- structure:
  - .bdg asset fields: [[asset]] subject, declared_subjects
  - .bdg entry fields: [[asset.entry]] kind, subject, zip_name, declared_subjects
  - .bdg non-runner step fields: op-local subject/path parameters
  - TOML runner-start recipes: [recipes.tests.<test_id>.steps.<step_index>]
Rules (normative):

statement:
- asset materialization data and non-runner step parameters MUST be read from the owning
  .bdg file; runner-start recipes MUST be read from the effective config selected by
  --config PATH

text:
- .bdg materialization data is the single source of truth for filesystem paths, subject
  bindings, zip entry targets, and non-runner step-level execution overrides; TOML
  runner-start recipes are the single source of truth for command-line arguments and
  runner-start execution overrides

statement:
- if a required subject declaration, asset materialization field, entry materialization
  field, or required runner-start step recipe is missing, the suite MUST FAIL
  deterministically before execution

- if .bdg-owned authority is repeated in TOML, or TOML-owned runner-start authority is
  repeated in .bdg, the suite MUST FAIL deterministically

text: - [evaluation] remains distinct and controls PASS/FAIL expectations centrally in TOML

statement: BadGuys MUST support this environment variable:

- AM_PATCH_BADGUYS_RUNNER_PYTHON
  - If set, and if runner_cmd[0] appears to be a Python executable selector
    (e.g., "python", "python3", "/usr/bin/python3", or a path ending in "/python" or "/python3"),
    then BadGuys MUST replace runner_cmd[0] with the value of AM_PATCH_BADGUYS_RUNNER_PYTHON.
  - Purpose: allow the runner to control the interpreter used for nested runner invocations
    when running inside environments where the default "python3" may not be valid.

text: Discovery is implemented in badguys/discovery.py.
Rules (normative):
1) Tests are files in badguys/tests/ matched by glob: *.bdg

statement: 2) Files MUST be processed in lexicographic order by filename.

3) Each test file MUST be a TOML document. The test id is the filename stem
   (basename without the .bdg extension).

4) The TOML document MAY contain a [meta] table. Supported keys:
   - makes_commit (bool, default false)
   - is_guard (bool, default false)

text: 5) The effective include filter is applied first (if non-empty).
6) The effective exclude filter is applied second.

statement:
7) If include and exclude are both non-empty and overlap, the suite MUST FAIL with:
   "FAIL: include/exclude conflict: <comma-separated names>"

8) If guard.require_guard_test=true:
   - if guard.guard_test_name is present in the effective exclude set, the suite MUST FAIL with:
     "FAIL: guard test excluded but required: <name>"
   - if the guard test is not present after filtering but exists in the full discovered set, the
     suite MUST inject the guard test back into the selected list.
   - if the guard test does not exist in the full discovered set, the suite MUST FAIL with:
     "FAIL: guard test not found: <name>"
   - the guard test MUST be moved to index 0 (run first)

text: - One test == one file "badguys/tests/*.bdg".
- The test id is the filename stem (basename without .bdg).

statement: - A .bdg file MUST be valid TOML and MUST contain at least one [[step]].

text:
The .bdg schema is defined by badguys/bdg_loader.py (normative), including [subjects.*],
asset-level materialization fields, entry-level materialization fields, and non-runner
step parameters:

- [meta] (optional table)
  - makes_commit (bool, default false)
  - is_guard (bool, default false)

- [[asset]] (optional array of tables)
  - id (string, required, unique within the file)
  - kind (string, required)
  - content (string, optional)
  - [[asset.entry]] (optional array of tables)
    - name (string, required)
    - content (string, required)

- [[step]] (required array of tables)
  - op (string, required)
  - additional keys are op-specific parameters

title: 7.1.1 Token substitution in strings (normative)
heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.1 Structure > 7.1.1 Token substitution in strings (normative)
BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.1 Structure > 7.1.1 Token substitution in strings (normative)
statement:
BadGuys MUST support token substitution in all string fields originating from a `.bdg` file,
including:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.1 Structure > 7.1.1 Token substitution in strings (normative)
text: - `[[asset]].content`

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.1 Structure > 7.1.1 Token substitution in strings (normative)
text: - `[[asset.entry]].content`

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.1 Structure > 7.1.1 Token substitution in strings (normative)
text: - any string parameter values inside `[[step]]` tables (including lists of strings)

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.1 Structure > 7.1.1 Token substitution in strings (normative)
statement: Tokens are written as `${name}` and MUST be replaced before an operation executes.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.1 Structure > 7.1.1 Token substitution in strings (normative)
text: Supported tokens (normative):

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.1 Structure > 7.1.1 Token substitution in strings (normative)
text: - `${issue_id}`: replaced with the effective `suite.issue_id` string.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.1 Structure > 7.1.1 Token substitution in strings (normative)
text:
- `${now_stamp}`: replaced with a per-test stamp computed once at test start,
  using local time format `YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS`.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.1 Structure > 7.1.1 Token substitution in strings (normative)
text: Consistency rule (normative):

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.1 Structure > 7.1.1 Token substitution in strings (normative)
statement: - Within a single test execution, every `${now_stamp}` expansion MUST yield the same value.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.1 Structure > 7.1.1 Token substitution in strings (normative)
text: Safety rule (normative):

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.1 Structure > 7.1.1 Token substitution in strings (normative)
statement:
- Central evaluation rules MUST NOT depend on the exact `${now_stamp}` value.
  `${now_stamp}` exists only to make prepared artifacts unique.

title: 7.2 Execution model
heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.2 Execution model
BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.2 Execution model
text: - Discovery produces TestDef objects whose run(ctx) returns a BdgTest.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.2 Execution model
statement: - The engine MUST execute the listed BdgStep objects in order.

title: 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
statement:
When a step executes the AM Patch Runner (scripts/am_patch.py), BadGuys MUST determine the
runner outcome primarily from the IPC socket event stream.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
text: Primary rule:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
statement:
- If an IPC event with type="result" is obtained, BadGuys MUST use:
  - ok (boolean) as the authoritative PASS/FAIL signal, and
  - return_code as the authoritative runner return code.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
text: Fallback rule:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
statement: - If no IPC type="result" event is obtained, BadGuys MUST fall back to the subprocess exit code.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
statement: BadGuys MUST NOT use stdout/stderr parsing as the authoritative decision source for PASS/FAIL.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
text: IPC stream persistence rule:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
statement:
- For every step that invokes scripts/am_patch.py, BadGuys MUST capture the complete IPC event
  stream (NDJSON, one JSON object per line) and write it into the per-test artifacts directory as:
  logs_dir/<test_id>/runner.ipc.step<step_index>.jsonl

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
statement:
- The stream file MUST contain the exact NDJSON lines received from the socket (no filtering and
  no reformatting).

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
text: Runner value_text rule:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
statement:
- For every runner-invoking step, BadGuys MUST compute a deterministic value_text string by
  concatenating the 'msg' field from every IPC event with type="log" in receive order, separated
  by a single '\n'. This value_text MUST be used as the step's evaluation 'value'.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
text: Runner artifact copy rule:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
statement:
- BadGuys MUST persist a structured terminal result object derived from the IPC `type="result"` event into:
  `logs_dir/<test_id>/runner.result.json`

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
statement: - The file `runner.result.json` MUST contain exactly one JSON object and MUST NOT contain NDJSON stream content.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
statement:
- If the IPC result includes `json_path`, BadGuys MUST copy that runner-owned NDJSON file into:
  `logs_dir/<test_id>/runner.log.jsonl`

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
statement:
- If the IPC result includes `log_path`:
  - if `suite.copy_runner_log=true`, BadGuys MAY copy it into `logs_dir/<test_id>/runner.log.txt`
  - otherwise, BadGuys MUST NOT copy it.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
statement: - The copy/write operation for `runner.result.json`, `runner.log.jsonl`, and `runner.log.txt` MUST occur eagerly when the valid IPC `type="result"` event is received, not after the runner subprocess exits.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
statement: - After eager persistence succeeds, BadGuys MUST treat the copied artifacts in `logs_dir/<test_id>/` as authoritative and MUST NOT require the original source path to remain present after runner exit.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
text: Artifact semantics (normative):

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
text: - `runner.result.json` = structured terminal result only.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
text: - `runner.log.jsonl` = runner-owned NDJSON log copied from `json_path`.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
text: - `runner.ipc.step<step_index>.jsonl` = exact raw IPC stream captured from the socket.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative)
text: - `runner.log.txt` = optional human-readable runner log copied from `log_path`.

title: 7.3.1 Required AMP-facing observability primitives (normative)
heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative) > 7.3.1 Required AMP-facing observability primitives (normative)
BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative) > 7.3.1 Required AMP-facing observability primitives (normative)
statement:
To satisfy the capability groups in section 1.1 without moving expectations
into `.bdg` files, BadGuys MUST provide the following observability primitives,
whether implemented as step operations, executor hooks, or equivalent centrally
controlled mechanisms:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative) > 7.3.1 Required AMP-facing observability primitives (normative)
statement:
- text file read
  - MUST read a deterministic UTF-8 text artifact from either:
    - a repo-relative path, or
    - a path relative to the current test artifacts directory
  - intended use: runner result JSON, generated manifests, or copied artifacts

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative) > 7.3.1 Required AMP-facing observability primitives (normative)
statement:
- zip entry listing
  - MUST return the sorted entry names from a zip artifact addressed by
    repo-relative path or test-artifact-relative path
  - intended use: archive hygiene and bundle layout assertions

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative) > 7.3.1 Required AMP-facing observability primitives (normative)
statement:
- git status inspection
  - MUST support at least two scopes: repo_root and runner workspace
  - MUST return deterministic porcelain-style paths for the selected scope
  - intended use: workspace, promotion, and finalize semantics

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative) > 7.3.1 Required AMP-facing observability primitives (normative)
statement:
- IPC command send
  - MUST send a newline-delimited JSON command to the runner IPC socket used by
    the current step
  - reply and control events observed after the command MUST remain available
    to central evaluation via the persisted IPC stream

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.3 Runner result determination (normative) > 7.3.1 Required AMP-facing observability primitives (normative)
statement:
These primitives are observability-only. They MUST NOT move command lines or PASS/FAIL
expectations out of TOML, and they MUST NOT move .bdg-owned subject/path/materialization
authority out of the owning .bdg file.

title: 7.4 Runner test mode (normative)
heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.4 Runner test mode (normative)
BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.4 Runner test mode (normative)
statement: When a step executes the AM Patch Runner, BadGuys MUST control runner mode centrally.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.4 Runner test mode (normative)
text: Default rule:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.4 Runner test mode (normative)
statement:
- For every step that invokes scripts/am_patch.py, BadGuys MUST inject the flag --test-mode
  into the runner argv (including informational invocations such as -h, --help-all, or --show-config).

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.4 Runner test mode (normative)
text: Full runner allowlist rule:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.4 Runner test mode (normative)
statement:
- If the current test_id is listed in config table [runner].full_runner_tests, BadGuys MUST NOT inject
  --test-mode for that test.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.4 Runner test mode (normative)
text: Single source of truth rule:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.4 Runner test mode (normative)
statement: - A test definition MUST NOT attempt to control runner mode directly via extra_args.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.4 Runner test mode (normative)
statement:
- If extra_args contains --test-mode for any runner invocation, the suite MUST FAIL before execution
  with a deterministic error message identifying the test_id.

title: 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative)
heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative)
BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative)
text: BadGuys provides two cleanup layers:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative)
text:
1) Engine cleanup between tests (hard isolation):
   - patches/workspaces/issue_<issue_id>/
   - patches/logs/issue_<issue_id>*
   - patches/successful/issue_<issue_id>*
   - patches/unsuccessful/issue_<issue_id>*
   - patches/patched_issue<issue_id>_*.zip
   - patches/issue_<issue_id>__bdg__*
   - patches/badguys_artifacts/issue_<issue_id>/

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative)
statement:
2) Per-plan cleanup_paths:
   - each path in cleanup_paths MUST be deleted after the test plan finishes (pass or fail)

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative)
statement: Tests MUST NOT rely on artifacts from any previous test.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative)
text:
Effective suite_jail semantics (normative):
- if effective suite_jail=true, BadGuys MUST prepare a writable issue-scoped jail repo at patches/badguys_suite_jail/issue_<issue_id>/repo and MUST execute the suite body inside bwrap against that repo
- if effective suite_jail=false, BadGuys MUST execute the suite body against the live repo root and MUST NOT require bwrap
- the BadGuys suite jail MUST NOT use quarantine/
- patches/badguys_suite_jail/issue_<issue_id>/ MUST be deleted after the entire suite run finishes (pass, fail, or interruption)
- if effective suite_jail=true and bwrap is unavailable, BadGuys MUST FAIL deterministically before suite execution begins
- in jailed mode, only log-class artifacts for the current run MAY remain in live host patches/: the central log at central_log_pattern and per-test artifacts under logs_dir/, subject to per_run_logs_post_run
- in both jailed and non-jailed modes, BadGuys MUST NOT leave any other current-issue live patches/ artifacts behind after suite exit

title: 7.5.1 Subject-scoped workspace preparation (normative)
heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative) > 7.5.1 Subject-scoped workspace preparation (normative)
BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative) > 7.5.1 Subject-scoped workspace preparation (normative)
statement:
BadGuys MAY provide engine-level workspace preparation steps that operate on declared
subjects before runner execution.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative) > 7.5.1 Subject-scoped workspace preparation (normative)
text: DELETE_SUBJECT semantics (normative):

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative) > 7.5.1 Subject-scoped workspace preparation (normative)
statement:
- A step with `op = "DELETE_SUBJECT"` MUST resolve its target exclusively from:
  - `recipes.tests.<test_id>.steps.<step_index>.subject`, and
  - `subjects.tests.<test_id>.<subject_id>.relpath`

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative) > 7.5.1 Subject-scoped workspace preparation (normative)
statement: - The `.bdg` step for `DELETE_SUBJECT` MUST NOT embed filesystem path parameters.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative) > 7.5.1 Subject-scoped workspace preparation (normative)
statement: - The step recipe for `DELETE_SUBJECT` MUST allow exactly one key: `subject`.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative) > 7.5.1 Subject-scoped workspace preparation (normative)
statement: - If the referenced subject is missing, the suite MUST FAIL deterministically.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative) > 7.5.1 Subject-scoped workspace preparation (normative)
statement: - If the resolved path does not exist, the operation MUST succeed with `rc=0`.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative) > 7.5.1 Subject-scoped workspace preparation (normative)
statement:
- If the resolved path is a regular file or a symlink, the operation MUST delete it and
  succeed with `rc=0`.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative) > 7.5.1 Subject-scoped workspace preparation (normative)
statement: - If the resolved path is a directory, the operation MUST FAIL deterministically.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.5 Cleanup and isolation (normative) > 7.5.1 Subject-scoped workspace preparation (normative)
statement: - DELETE_SUBJECT MUST NOT target any path that is not declared in the owning .bdg [subjects] table.

title: 7.6 Forbidden patterns
heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.6 Forbidden patterns
BadGuys Specification > 7. Test contract > 7.6 Forbidden patterns
statement:
- A test MUST NOT invoke "python3 badguys/badguys.py" (directly or indirectly).
  Reason: the suite holds a global lock during the run; nesting would deadlock or violate isolation.

title: 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat
heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat

title: 8.1 Log outputs
heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.1 Log outputs
BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.1 Log outputs
statement: BadGuys MUST produce:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.1 Log outputs
BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.1 Log outputs
statement:
- per-test artifacts at: logs_dir/<test_id>/ (directory)
  - each test directory MUST contain a file: badguys.test.jsonl (NDJSON)
  - for each runner-invoking step, the directory MUST contain the corresponding
    runner.ipc.step<step_index>.jsonl file (see 7.3)

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.1 Log outputs
statement:
The per-test artifacts root directory (logs_dir) MUST be cleared at the start of each suite run.
The central log path MUST be created (parents created as needed).

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.1 Log outputs
text: BadGuys has three distinct verbosity controls:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.1 Log outputs
text: - runner verbosity: affects the nested AM Patch Runner invocation (passed as --verbosity=<mode>)

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.1 Log outputs
text: - console verbosity: affects what BadGuys prints to the terminal

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.1 Log outputs
text: - log verbosity: affects what BadGuys writes into logs (central + per-test)

title: 8.3 Console behavior (normative)
heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.3 Console behavior (normative)
BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.3 Console behavior (normative)
statement:
- console_verbosity=quiet:
  - MUST suppress progress output
  - MUST disable heartbeat

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.3 Console behavior (normative)
statement:
- console_verbosity=normal:
  - MUST print per-test result lines as tests complete
  - MUST NOT print step completion lines
  - MUST NOT print the debug configuration line
  - MUST NOT print live runner log lines
  - heartbeat MUST be enabled for long-running command execution (see 8.4)
- console_verbosity=verbose:
  - MUST print everything required for normal except the three MUST NOT clauses above
  - MUST additionally print one step completion line for each executed step using exactly this format:
    - "BadGuys step: test=<test_id> step=<step_index> op=<op> rc=<rc>"
  - MUST NOT print the debug configuration line
  - MUST NOT print live runner log lines
  - heartbeat MUST be enabled for long-running command execution (see 8.4)
- console_verbosity=debug:
  - MUST print everything required for verbose
  - MUST additionally print exactly one effective configuration line at run start using exactly this format:
    - "BadGuys debug: config=<config_path> console=<console_verbosity> log=<log_verbosity> issue=<issue_id> per_run_logs_post_run=<mode> suite_jail=<true|false> runner_cmd=<runner_cmd>"
  - for each RUN_RUNNER step, MUST mirror every runner IPC event with:
    - type="log"
    - msg present and string-typed
  - each mirrored runner log line MUST be written to stdout immediately upon receipt as exactly:
    - "<msg>\n"
  - mirrored runner log lines MUST preserve receive order
  - mirrored runner log lines MUST NOT add any prefix, suffix, severity, channel, JSON wrapper, or other metadata
  - non-log IPC events MUST NOT be mirrored to the console
  - raw subprocess stdout/stderr artifacts MUST NOT be mirrored by this rule
  - if a RUN_RUNNER step emitted at least one mirrored runner log line, all mirrored runner log lines for that step MUST appear before that step's:
    - "BadGuys step: test=<test_id> step=<step_index> op=<op> rc=<rc>"
  - heartbeat MUST be enabled for long-running command execution (see 8.4)

title: Summary line (normative)
heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.3 Console behavior (normative) > Summary line (normative)
BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.3 Console behavior (normative) > Summary line (normative)
statement:
- console_verbosity=quiet:
  - MUST print exactly one final summary line:
    - "BadGuys summary: OK" or "BadGuys summary: FAIL"
  - MUST NOT include passed/failed counts

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.3 Console behavior (normative) > Summary line (normative)
statement:
- console_verbosity in {normal, verbose, debug}:
  - MUST print a final summary line that includes counts:
    - "BadGuys summary: OK passed=<N> failed=<M>" or
    - "BadGuys summary: FAIL passed=<N> failed=<M>"
  - passed MUST equal the number of executed tests that PASSED
  - failed MUST equal the number of executed tests that FAILED
  - tests that did not execute (e.g. interrupted run) MUST NOT be counted as failed

title: 8.4 Heartbeat (normative)
heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.4 Heartbeat (normative)
BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.4 Heartbeat (normative)
statement: - Heartbeat MUST be emitted only when console_verbosity in {normal, verbose, debug}.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.4 Heartbeat (normative)
statement: - Heartbeat MUST be written to stderr.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.4 Heartbeat (normative)
statement: - Heartbeat cadence SHOULD be approximately every 5 seconds while a command step runs.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 8. Logging, verbosity, and heartbeat > 8.4 Heartbeat (normative)
statement: - Heartbeat content MUST indicate that the suite is still running a test step (exact wording is implementation-defined).
BadGuys MUST use a process lock to prevent concurrent runs.

text: Ordering (normative):

statement: - acquire_lock MUST happen before initializing/clearing logs and before executing any tests.
- release_lock MUST happen in a finally-equivalent block so it runs even on failures/exceptions.
Lock file content (text) MUST contain:

text: - pid=<int>
- started=<unix-epoch-seconds>
Behavior:

statement: - on_conflict=fail: the second run MUST FAIL if lock exists
- on_conflict=steal: the second run MAY steal the lock only if stale by ttl_seconds, otherwise MUST FAIL

title: 10. Commit tests and commit_limit
heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 10. Commit tests and commit_limit
BadGuys Specification > 10. Commit tests and commit_limit
text: A test is a commit test if TEST["makes_commit"] == True.

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 10. Commit tests and commit_limit
statement: Before executing tests, BadGuys MUST check:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 10. Commit tests and commit_limit
statement: - if the number of selected commit tests > commit_limit, the suite MUST FAIL before executing any test steps

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 10. Commit tests and commit_limit
statement: The FAIL message SHOULD include:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 10. Commit tests and commit_limit
text: - how many commit tests were selected

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 10. Commit tests and commit_limit
text: - the configured/effective commit_limit

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 10. Commit tests and commit_limit
text: - the list of selected commit tests

title: 11. Error reporting and determinism
heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 11. Error reporting and determinism
BadGuys Specification > 11. Error reporting and determinism
statement: When a failure happens, the suite MUST:

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 11. Error reporting and determinism
text: - return non-zero exit code

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 11. Error reporting and determinism
text: - emit a FAIL reason that is deterministic and actionable

heading_path: BadGuys Specification > 11. Error reporting and determinism
statement: All paths in messages SHOULD be repo-relative where feasible.

BadGuys MAY add new configuration keys and new CLI options in the future.
Any such additions MUST be specified in this document (including defaults and precedence) before being considered normative.

text: BadGuys tests are defined by files under `badguys/tests/` with the `.bdg` extension.
A `.bdg` file is TOML and contains:
- `[meta]` with optional `makes_commit` (bool) and `is_guard` (bool)
- `[[asset]]` entries with `id`, `kind`, and embedded content
- `[[asset.entry]]` entries with logical entry ids and embedded content
- `[[step]]` entries with `op` and parameters.

statement:
Logical subject-to-path mapping, asset materialization, entry materialization, and
non-runner step parameters MUST live in the owning .bdg file. Runner-start recipes MUST
live in badguys/config.toml under [recipes.tests.<test_id>.steps.<step_index>].

.bdg MUST NOT contain runner-start command lines, runner-start overrides, or PASS/FAIL
expectations that are controlled by TOML. TOML MUST NOT contain .bdg-owned filesystem
paths, subject bindings, asset materialization, entry materialization, or non-runner
step overrides.

text: All PASS/FAIL rules remain defined centrally in badguys/config.toml under [evaluation] and keyed by (test_id, step_index).
If `evaluation.strict_coverage=true`, missing rules for a step is a deterministic FAIL.
Supported evaluation rule keys (normative):
- rc_eq (int), rc_ne (int)

- stdout_contains (string|list[string]), stdout_not_contains (string|list[string]),
  stdout_regex (string|list[string])

- stderr_contains (string|list[string]), stderr_not_contains (string|list[string]),
  stderr_regex (string|list[string])

- value_eq (any), value_contains (string|list[string]), value_not_contains (string|list[string]),
  value_regex (string|list[string])

- list_eq (list[string]), list_contains (string|list[string]),
  list_not_contains (string|list[string])
- equals_step_index (int)
Runner evaluation constraint (normative):

statement:
- For any step that invokes scripts/am_patch.py, evaluation rules MUST NOT use any stdout_* or
  stderr_* keys. The runner subprocess stdio is non-authoritative and may be empty.

- For any step that invokes scripts/am_patch.py, the evaluation 'value' MUST be the runner
  value_text defined in section 7.3.
